# Schema: Ticket Breakdown
<!-- Defines the structure of tickets.md generated by /pm-handoff tickets. -->
<!-- Two categories: Features (SMART) and User Stories (standard agile). -->
<!-- Designed to be copy-paste ready for Linear, Jira, or any issue tracker. -->

Tickets are written to `features/[feature-name]/tickets.md`.

---

## Category 1: Features (SMART Format)

Features define what the product will deliver — scoped, outcome-oriented, and time-bound. Write one Feature ticket per major deliverable.

```markdown
### FEAT-[###]: [Feature Title]

**Specific:** [Exactly what will be built — no ambiguity. One sentence.]
**Measurable:** [How success will be measured — metric and target. E.g., "Reduce time-to-first-result by 40%"]
**Achievable:** [Why this is in scope — note key constraints or dependencies that make it feasible]
**Relevant:** [Why this matters now — ties to a product goal, user need, or business priority]
**Time-bound:** [Target delivery date or sprint]

**Priority:** P0 | P1 | P2
**Epic:** [Parent initiative or OKR]
**Labels:** [frontend | backend | design | data | infrastructure]
```

---

## Category 2: User Stories (Standard Agile)

User stories define user-facing value. Write one Story per distinct user action or outcome. Each story must be independently deliverable.

```markdown
### STORY-[###]: [Story Title]

**As a** [specific user segment — not "user"],
**I want to** [action or capability],
**so that** [outcome or benefit the user gets].

**Priority:** P0 | P1 | P2
**Estimate:** XS | S | M | L | XL
**Epic:** [parent feature or FEAT-###]

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] [Testable criterion 1 — observable behavior, not implementation detail]
- [ ] [Testable criterion 2]
- [ ] [Testable criterion 3]

**Out of scope for this story:**
- [Explicit exclusion — prevents scope creep during implementation]

**Dependencies:** [Ticket IDs or teams that must complete first, or "None"]
**Labels:** [frontend | backend | design | data | infrastructure]
```

---

## Structure Rules

- Every STORY must belong to a FEAT
- Every FEAT must have at least one STORY
- Spikes (research tasks) use STORY format with "Investigate:" prefix in the title
- Tasks that are non-user-facing (infra, migrations) use FEAT format only

---

## Linear Export Format

| ProdMan field | FEAT → Linear | STORY → Linear |
|---------------|--------------|----------------|
| Title | title | title |
| Specific / As a… | description | description |
| Priority | priority (1=Urgent, 2=High, 3=Medium, 4=Low) | priority |
| Time-bound / Estimate | dueDate | estimate |
| Labels | labelNames | labelNames |
| Epic | type = Feature (top-level) | parentId → FEAT ticket |
| Acceptance Criteria | — | acceptance criteria (custom field) |

---

## Jira Export Format

| ProdMan field | FEAT → Jira | STORY → Jira |
|---------------|------------|--------------|
| Title | Summary | Summary |
| Specific / As a… | Description | Description |
| Priority | Priority | Priority |
| Time-bound / Estimate | Due Date | Story Points |
| Labels | Labels | Labels |
| Epic | Issue Type = Epic | Epic Link → FEAT ticket |
| Acceptance Criteria | — | Acceptance Criteria (custom field) |

---

## Example Ticket Set

```markdown
# Order History Search — Tickets

## Features

### FEAT-001: Order History Keyword Search

**Specific:** Add a keyword search field to the Orders page that filters by order number, item name, or seller name.
**Measurable:** Reduce "can't find order" support tickets by 30% within 60 days of launch.
**Achievable:** Search scope limited to existing order data fields — no new data model changes required.
**Relevant:** Top support ticket category for the past 3 months; directly impacts buyer retention.
**Time-bound:** Sprint ending 2026-04-15

**Priority:** P0
**Epic:** Buyer Experience Q2
**Labels:** frontend, backend

---

## User Stories

### STORY-001: Search orders by keyword

**As a** returning buyer,
**I want to** search my past orders by keyword,
**so that** I can find a specific order without scrolling through my entire history.

**Priority:** P0
**Estimate:** M
**Epic:** FEAT-001

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Search input is visible on the Orders page above the order list
- [ ] Typing filters results within 300ms (debounced)
- [ ] Search matches on order number, item name, and seller name
- [ ] Empty state is shown when no results match
- [ ] Clearing the input restores the full order list

**Out of scope for this story:**
- Do not add search to the Order Detail page
- Do not persist search state across page refreshes

**Dependencies:** FEAT-001 backend index (STORY-002)
**Labels:** frontend

---

### STORY-002: Index order data for search

**As a** backend system,
**I want to** index order records on order_number, item_name, and seller_name,
**so that** keyword search queries return results within acceptable latency.

**Priority:** P0
**Estimate:** S
**Epic:** FEAT-001

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Index created on order_number, item_name, seller_name fields
- [ ] Query p95 latency < 200ms
- [ ] Existing orders backfilled into the index on deploy
- [ ] New orders indexed on creation

**Out of scope for this story:**
- Full-text search ranking or relevance scoring

**Dependencies:** None
**Labels:** backend, infrastructure
```
